notes.text: What We Know Daddy saw the actual marble shot, not just the same daily layout. That should be impossible in the current /Users/CassandraMyers/Developer/SparkleReef code because Daily Bubble does not send shot angle, board state, marble movement, queue index, or camera position anywhere. Current code only syncs Daily Bubble result summaries: best score, cleared, attempts, note, sparkle. What I Investigated I checked: Daily Bubble view/model/engine Firebase Daily Bubble sync shared game routing Firebase active game rules old CloudKit share code SwiftData CloudKit model container Xcode prod/dev schemes other Sparkle Reef folders on the Mac I found no current code path that would live-sync a Daily Bubble shot. Possible Explanations Most likely: TestFlight did not contain the code I inspected, or the uploaded build came from another Sparkle Reef folder/project. There are multiple copies, including review/audit folders, and the app still shows 1.0 build 1, so it is hard to verify exactly what code is installed. Also possible: a stale TestFlight build or local installed app was tested instead of the newest archive. Less likely, but still worth hardening: Daily Bubble is still listed as allowed in shared Firebase active game rules, even though the app does not currently start it as a shared game. CloudKit/SwiftData can sync Daily Bubble result rows, sparkles, and jar data, but I did not find evidence that it can sync live marble shots. Recommended Fix Path Later Add a visible build fingerprint on the home screen, e.g. commit hash/date. Increment build number before every TestFlight upload. Re-archive only from /Users/CassandraMyers/Developer/SparkleReef/SparkleReef.xcodeproj using SparkleReef-Prod. Remove dailyBubble from shared active-game rules and app-level shared-game eligibility. Make Daily Bubble explicitly local gameplay, with Firebase used only for per-role daily result comparison. Add tests proving two Daily Bubble instances for the same day start with the same pearls but mutate independently after one player shoots. Current Confidence I’m high-confidence the current inspected code should not live-sync Daily Bubble shots. I’m medium-high confidence the real issue is build/source confusion or stale TestFlight code. I’m high-confidence Daily Bubble should still be hardened so it cannot ever accidentally enter shared-session behavior.   9:03 PM

runbook.title: The stages every app climbs
runbook.when: the blueprint · read once, follow always

steps.why: guardrails before features, so quality is never retrofitted into an audit later
steps.check: you watched the canary test fail on purpose, then delete it and go green

steps.why: what it is, and the more important list of what it is NOT; drift is fifty small inconsistent decisions
steps.check: SPEC.md says LOCKED at the top and is committed

steps.why: a design system that is not machine-enforced regresses to grey stock cards every sprint
steps.check: a raw hex colour or magic font size is rejected by the quality hook

steps.why: one thing all the way through, so you learn the whole loop works on day one, not week three
steps.check: one item, end to end, screenshotted on your real phone

steps.why: now the rest of the screens; anything that interrupts you (notifications) is built last and never loud
steps.check: device, every time

steps.why: CloudKit, development container only, always
steps.check: it survives a delete and reinstall

steps.why: things move, they do not teleport; Dynamic Type, VoiceOver, reduced motion; delete dead code
steps.check: usable with VoiceOver, with your eyes closed

steps.why: TestFlight, then the App Store
steps.check: someone who is not you installs it and opens it

runbook.title: Build one queue item
runbook.when: the core loop · 90% of the work

steps.why: dependencies mean you never build on a missing prerequisite
steps.check: it names ONE item (the top of Up next) and what 'done' means

steps.why: you review intent, not code
steps.how: plan the next item, no code yet
steps.check: a short plan you understand and approve

steps.do: Test first — watch it go RED
steps.why: a test written after the code just blesses the bug
steps.how: write the failing test for it, run it, show me red
steps.check: you see a genuinely failing test

steps.why: the Stop hook will not let the turn end while verify is red
steps.check: verify: green (format, lint, build, test)

steps.why: true in code is not true on device
steps.how: capture a simulator screenshot + accessibility report
steps.check: the screenshot shows what you meant

steps.why: git + the board are the truth
steps.how: commit (with 'Closes #<n>'), move the item to 🔍 Verify on the board, add a CHANGELOG line
steps.check: the item sits in Verify awaiting your device check; you close it → Done

runbook.title: Stand up a new app (the factory)

steps.why: the factory is the machine that builds every app with the guardrails already inside. You set it up a single time and reuse it forever.
steps.how: Unzip _bootstrap into your Developer folder so it sits at ~/Developer/_bootstrap. You never do this step again.
steps.check: ~/Developer/_bootstrap contains a 'newapp' file and a 'template' folder

steps.why: this stamps out the app's skeleton — safety hooks, tests, the verify gate — and now also creates its private GitHub repo + Roadmap board and registers it in Tiara, all before any code exists
steps.check: Terminal shows '<AppName> scaffolded' plus [done] lines for the repo + board; the app appears in Tiara with its own board

steps.why: no script can make an Xcode project honestly, so this is the one hands-on part
steps.how: Xcode → File > New > Project → iOS App → SwiftUI + SwiftData, save INTO ~/Developer/<AppName>, then right-click Sources and Tests → Convert to Folder. Then set up two build configs: Debug = DEV (bundle id ending .dev, home-screen name '<AppName> DEV', development CloudKit) and Release = the clean app — so both install on your phone side by side.
steps.check: adding a file on disk shows up in the build with no project-file change, and a DEV build installs next to the real one

steps.do: Watch the canary fail, then go green
steps.why: a test suite that was never wired up says 'all passed' forever. Watching it fail once, on purpose, proves the tests really run.
steps.check: you SEE a red failing test, then delete CanaryTests.swift and it goes green

steps.why: the spec is the part only you decide. Claude writes it in the right format, then seeds your first items onto the GitHub board so you never touch the format.
steps.prompt: Read SPEC.md, docs/BLUEPRINT.md, CLAUDE.md, and the ADRs in docs/decisions. Then help me fill in SPEC.md for <AppName> — ask me ONE question at a time: what it is in a paragraph, what it is NOT, the data, the screens, and what "done" means. Write my answers into SPEC.md in its existing format and keep it DRAFT until I say LOCKED. Then add my first items to the GitHub board the factory already created for this app: one issue per stage, each added to the board with Lane = Up next and a priority + type. Do not write any app code yet.
steps.check: SPEC.md says LOCKED, and your first items appear on the board (and in Tiara)

steps.why: lock the look before any screens exist, so nothing turns into grey stock UI. The app icon lives here — it's a design choice, not plumbing.
steps.how: Ask Claude to add Tokens.swift + a lint rule that rejects raw colours and sizes. Then in Xcode drop a 1024×1024 PNG into Assets.xcassets → AppIcon, and add a second set 'AppIcon-Dev' with the same art plus a DEV ribbon (drop your icon into the Tiara Helper folder as icon.png and double-click 'Make DEV icon' to stamp the ribbon for you), pointed at by the Debug config.
steps.check: a raw hex colour is rejected by the hook; the real icon shows on Release and the ribboned DEV icon on Debug

steps.why: prove the whole loop works on day one
steps.how: run the Build recipe on the top item of your queue
steps.check: one feature green and seen on your device

runbook.title: Triage sweep (together)
runbook.when: weekly, or when the inbox fills

steps.why: dedup + sequencing at the door stops the swamp
steps.how: for each line: bin · duplicate · promote to the queue (with depends-on)
steps.check: inbox empty; new items sit ordered in the queue
